title: Understanding Frictional Force
text: The frictional force can be calculated using the formula: frictional force $$=$$ coefficient of static friction * normal force.

title: Calculate the Normal Force
text: The normal force is equal to the weight supported by the knee. Use the formula: weight $$=$$ mass * gravitational acceleration (g $$=$$ $$9.8$$ m/s²).

title: Calculate the Weight Supported by the Knee
text: Calculate the weight supported by the knee using the mass of $$66.0$$ kg and gravitational acceleration of $$9.8$$ m/s².

title: Calculate the Maximum Frictional Force
text: Use the coefficient of static friction $$(0.016)$$ and the normal force $$(646.8$$ N) to find the maximum frictional force.
